name: Stage 1 — Comprehensive Check
description: Every participant reads the same fixed financials and answers four fact-based multiple-choice questions. There is one unambiguous right answer for each — this is the AI-favorable control: the task is pure look-up / rule-based reasoning, so an individual with AI (or fully-automatic AI) is expected to match a team. All four questions are parallel and auto-assigned; nobody has to claim them.
stage_manipulation: The task is fact-based and has unambiguous right answers.

tasks.name: Paid users
tasks.chat_context_override: The financials brief lists 20 paid users.
tasks.hitl_prompt: From the financials brief, select how many **paid** users the startup has.

comp_users.label: How many paid users does the startup have?

tasks.name: Monthly price
tasks.chat_context_override: The brief charges $2/month per paid user.
tasks.hitl_prompt: Select the monthly price charged per paid user.

comp_price.label: What is the monthly price per paid user?

tasks.name: Monthly fixed cost
tasks.chat_context_override: The brief lists a $1,000/month fixed cost.
tasks.hitl_prompt: Select the startup's fixed monthly cost.

comp_cost.label: What is the monthly fixed cost?

tasks.name: Monthly burn
tasks.chat_context_override: The monthly burn works out to $6,530.
tasks.hitl_prompt: Select the startup's monthly burn (operating loss). Remember variable cost applies to all 1,080 active users, free and paid.

context_brief_template: You are checking the fixed unit economics of a startup with 1,000 free users, 80 paid users at $20/month, $6,000/month fixed cost, and $2 variable cost per active user. Each question has one correct answer that can be read off or computed directly from the brief.
